name: Oubachu Trail
name_original: Oubachu — circular desde Centro de Interpretación de Muniellos
region: National Park Fuentes del Narcea Degaña e Ibias
description: The Oubachu Trail is a moderate 4.1-mile touring cycling route in Asturias, offering views of oak forests and the village of Oubachu.
region_intro: Touring cycling around National Park Fuentes del Narcea Degaña e Ibias offers routes through a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve in southwestern Asturias, Spain. The region features mountainous terrain with rugged ridges and deep valleys carved by the Narcea and Ibias rivers. Extensive beech and oak forests, including the Muniellos oak forest, cover much of the landscape. The park's highest peak is Cueto de Arbas at 2,002 meters. Best touring cycling routes around National Park Fuentes del Narcea Degaña e Ibias The most popular touring cycling route is Oubachu Trail, a 4.1 miles (6.6 km) moderate trail that takes 41 minutes to complete. This route offers a moderate challenge through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Santuario de Nuestra Señora del Acebo — circular desde Puenticiella, a difficult 11.0 miles (17.7 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain and panoramic vistas. Local touring cyclists also love the Pereda de Ancares loop from Ermida da Madalena, a 29.7 miles (47.8 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain and forests, often completed in about 3 hours 18 minutes. Touring cycling around National Park Fuentes del Narcea Degaña e Ibias is defined by mountainous terrain, deep river valleys, and extensive beech and oak forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to challenging routes with significant gradients. The routes in National Park Fuentes del Narcea Degaña e Ibias are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.9 stars from over 8 reviews. More than 280 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore National Park Fuentes del Narcea Degaña e Ibias's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in National Park Fuentes del Narcea Degaña e Ibias?
A: There are over 25 touring cycling routes available in National Park Fuentes del Narcea Degaña e Ibias, offering a variety of experiences through its mountainous terrain and extensive forests.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of touring cycling routes in this park?
A: The touring cycling routes in National Park Fuentes del Narcea Degaña e Ibias are predominantly challenging, with 24 out of 27 routes rated as difficult. There are also 2 moderate routes and 1 easy route available, providing options for different skill levels. Expect significant elevation gains and gradients on many paths.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see while cycling in National Park Fuentes del Narcea Degaña e Ibias?
A: While cycling, you'll encounter a stunning array of natural features. The park is characterized by rugged ridges, deep valleys carved by the Narcea and Ibias rivers, and extensive, well-preserved forests, including vast beech and oak groves. The rhythmic sound of water from the river network is a constant companion on many routes.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in natural landmarks. You can cycle past significant mountain passes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5593878" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo de las Mujeres Muertas Pass&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5593872" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Connio Pass&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5371946" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Leitariegos&lt;/a&gt;, which offer breathtaking views. The park also features interpretation centers, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6276468" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muniellos Interpretation Center&lt;/a&gt;, providing insights into the area's heritage.

Q: Can I spot wildlife while touring cycling in the park?
A: National Park Fuentes del Narcea, Degaña e Ibias is a vital sanctuary for wildlife. While cycling, you might have the chance to spot roe deer, wild boar, and various forest birds. It's also home to the endangered Cantabrian brown bear and Cantabrian capercaillie, though sightings of these more elusive animals are less common.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, there are several circular touring cycling routes. For example, the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1370435951/santuario-de-nuestra-senora-del-acebo-circular-desde-puenticiella" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santuario de Nuestra Señora del Acebo — circular desde Puenticiella&lt;/a&gt; offers significant elevation gain and panoramic vistas. Another option is the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38146296" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pereda de Ancares loop from Ermida da Madalena&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through mountainous terrain and forests.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for families with children?
A: While many routes in the park are rated as difficult due to the mountainous terrain and significant gradients, there are some easier options. The park offers 1 easy and 2 moderate routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1369842913/oubachu-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oubachu Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 4.1-mile path. These might be more suitable for families depending on their experience and fitness levels.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in National Park Fuentes del Narcea Degaña e Ibias?
A: The best time for touring cycling in this mountainous region is typically from late spring to early autumn (May to October). During these months, the weather is generally more stable, and the mountain passes are clear of snow. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out, as conditions can change rapidly in mountainous areas.

Q: Is public transport available to access touring cycling routes in the park?
A: Access to the more remote areas of National Park Fuentes del Narcea Degaña e Ibias, where many cycling routes are located, can be challenging via public transport. It is advisable to plan your journey with a private vehicle or consider local taxi services if you intend to reach specific trailheads.

Q: Are there options for parking near the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in the villages and near popular trailheads within and around the National Park. Mountain passes like Puerto de Leitariegos and Puerto de Cerredo also serve as ideal stopover points with amenities, including parking, making them good starting points for rides.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about their experience in National Park Fuentes del Narcea Degaña e Ibias?
A: The touring cycling routes in National Park Fuentes del Narcea Degaña e Ibias are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.9 stars from over 8 reviews. Cyclists often praise the stunning natural beauty, the challenge of the mountainous terrain, and the tranquility of the extensive forests.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that are particularly good for experiencing the park's forests?
A: The park is renowned for its extensive and well-preserved forests, including vast beech and oak woodlands. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38146296" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pereda de Ancares loop from Ermida da Madalena&lt;/a&gt; lead directly through these impressive forested areas, allowing you to immerse yourself in the natural beauty of the region's ancient trees.
tips: A curious building (an old restored quarry) camouflaged in the middle of the forest where the Muniellos Nature Reserve Interpretation Center is located, the most protected natural space in Asturias. You can get to know Muniellos and enjoy the cafeteria and recreational area.
A tiny chapel, almost hidden among trees. The nature that surrounds the place transmits peace and tranquility.
A small town, starting point for the visit in the Muniellos forest. In the upper part of the town you will find a fountain if you need to cool off.

faqs.question: Is the Oubachu Trail suitable for touring cycling?
faqs.answer: While the komoot tour data lists this as a touring cycling route, the Oubachu Trail (PR.AS-252) is primarily identified as a circular hiking route. It features narrow paths, uneven surfaces, and a challenging initial uphill climb, making it generally not well-suited for touring bicycles. For cyclists, routes like the "Senda del Oso" or road cycling in Picos de Europa might be more appropriate.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a varied terrain, including narrow paths, wider tracks, and some asphalted sections as it descends towards the village of Oubachu. The initial section from the Muniellos Interpretation Center is uphill and features a steep path through oak trees.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts near the Muniellos Interpretation Center in Cangas del Narcea, Asturias. Parking facilities are usually available at or near the interpretation center, serving as the main trailhead for this area.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers picturesque views of the dense oak forests of Muniellos, often called "Asturias' forest par excellence." You'll also see the charming village of Oubachu with its traditional Asturian 'hórreos' (granaries), the Oballo Chapel, and distant views of the Leitariegos mountains.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit?
faqs.answer: The trail traverses a rich Atlantic forest ecosystem. While beautiful year-round, spring and autumn offer vibrant colors and milder temperatures, making them ideal for exploring the beech, oak, and holly trees, as well as abundant ferns and moss.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, this specific trail (PR.AS-252) does not require special authorization or permits, unlike some other highly protected areas within the larger Muniellos region of the Fuentes del Narcea, Degaña, and Ibias Natural Park.

faqs.question: Is the Oubachu Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Information on specific dog regulations for this particular trail is not detailed. However, as it's located within a Natural Park, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all visitors. Always check local signage for any specific restrictions.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete the trail?
faqs.answer: For hikers, the Oubachu Trail is generally classified as an easy to moderate route, with a typical duration of 1 hour 45 minutes to 3 hours. The initial uphill climb can be challenging.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally classified as an "easy" to "moderate" hiking route. The most significant challenge is the initial uphill climb from the Muniellos Interpretation Center.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or services near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail starts near the Muniellos Interpretation Center, which typically offers visitor information and basic facilities. The nearby village of Oubachu might offer limited local services.

faqs.question: What kind of natural environment does the trail pass through?
faqs.answer: The trail is situated within the Fuentes del Narcea, Degaña, and Ibias Natural Park, immersing you in a rich Atlantic forest ecosystem. You'll experience dense oak forests, often referred to as "Asturias' forest par excellence," along with beech, holly trees, and abundant ferns and moss.
